reason: Core of this task: the #[tauri::command] + invoke/emit contract used to push 6119 poll results from Rust to the frontend; defines name/arg/return sync rules and Result<T,E> error pattern
Any new plugin (e.g. http) needs a capability in src-tauri/capabilities/default.json; explains how to grant the narrowest permission
Rust/Tauri layer layout and the rule to keep lib.rs thin; where the 6119 polling backend code lives
The React<->Rust IPC seam is this app's critical boundary; checklist for keeping command/invoke and serde/TS types in sync
Pattern for wrapping invoke and subscribing to Tauri events (listen) inside hooks with StrictMode-safe cleanup — used by the game-state poller and cue scheduler
useState-first, immutable updates, and event-listener cleanup rules for game-state and cue-scheduling state
Typing invoke<T>/event payloads and mirroring the Rust serde game-state shape as TS types; strict-mode constraints
Function-component/props/styling conventions for the always-on-top overlay UI
Where new components/hooks/lib and the build-order JSON data files should live
